content.problemStatement:
Forking means creating your own copy of someone else’s repository on GitHub so you can make changes without affecting the original project.

After forking, you clone your fork to your local machine to work on it.

---

**Fork on GitHub** 
1. **Go to the original repo** \
(Use this repo you used previously to clone : https://github.com/codechef8899/clone-practice-repo/) 


2. **Click the Fork button** → now the repo appears under your GitHub account

---

- Now you can clone your fork using `Git Clone`
- This creates a local copy of your fork

**You can now make changes, commit, and push freely to your fork**
